D6 Display L3 Apply Reference

IPS · TN · VA Off-Axis

tilt the screen and watch the panel type decide what survives — IPS holds, VA washes, TN inverts

Liquid-crystal panels twist light differently off-axis. IPS rotates crystals in-plane, so colour and contrast stay stable at wide angles — at the cost of slower response and "IPS glow". TN is fast and cheap but shifts colour badly and even inverts lightness when viewed from below. VA has the deepest blacks but its gamma shifts off-axis, washing shadows.

Choose a panel and tilt the viewing angle. The test patches degrade by the panel's signature: IPS barely moves, VA's shadows lift and wash, TN casts colour and finally inverts. The same content, three different off-axis fates.
